name: Pearl Harbor National Memorial
tagline: The USS Arizona Memorial, Battleship Missouri, and the site where December 7, 1941 changed the world — free admission, book ahead.
parking: $7/day in visitor center lot — arrives early, fills fast. Do NOT leave valuables in your car (high-theft area)
hours: Daily 7am–5pm. Last Arizona Memorial boat at 3:30pm. Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day
bestTime: Arrive by 7am for walk-up tickets or book online 8 weeks ahead on Recreation.gov ($1 service fee). Mornings are cooler and less crowded
talkStory: Four major sites, each takes a couple hours: the USS Arizona Memorial (free, 45-min program with documentary and boat ride to the memorial), USS Bowfin submarine, Battleship Missouri, and Pearl Harbor Aviation Museum (last two require bus to Ford Island, separate paid admission). NO BAGS ALLOWED — no purses, backpacks, camera bags, or diaper bags. Only wallets, cameras, phones, and water bottles. Bag storage available near entrance for $5-10. 1,300 free walk-up tickets released daily but go fast. The memorial straddles the sunken hull of the Arizona without touching it — you can still see oil leaking from the ship 80+ years later. Plan a full day if seeing all four sites.
